nativeRange.habitat: Deep reef and rocky areas at roughly 30–60 m depth; tends to shelter in caves, crevices, and overhangs on reef walls.

diet.notes: Has been documented accepting meaty marine foods in captivity, including mysis shrimp, marine fish flesh, crustacean flesh, and quality frozen preparations. Trophic level of 3.6 indicates a carnivorous/piscivorous diet in the wild.

avoidWith: very large predatory fish (lionfish, groupers, eels)

summary: The Caribbean Deepwater Cave Basslet (Liopropoma mowbrayi) is a small, vividly colored reef fish from deep Caribbean waters, generally considered peaceful and reef-safe. Shy by nature, it tends to settle best in tanks with caves, overhangs, and subdued lighting.
careNotes:
The Caribbean Deepwater Cave Basslet is generally regarded as one of the easier reef fish to keep, though its deep-water origins mean specimens tend to appreciate subdued lighting and well-aquascaped tanks with numerous caves, crevices, and overhangs. In the wild it inhabits reef walls and rocky habitats at 30–60 m depth, so acclimation can take some time — newly introduced fish may hide for extended periods before venturing into open water.

This species is generally considered peaceful toward most non-similar tankmates, but individuals have been known to be territorial toward conspecifics and other similarly shaped basslets. It is generally advised to keep only one per tank unless the system is quite large with multiple distinct cave territories.

A tight-fitting lid is advisable, as the species has a moderate tendency to jump.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (family Liopropomatidae, order Perciformes/Serranoidei), max size (9.0 cm TL), temperature range (20.9–27.5°C), native range (Western Atlantic: Bermuda, southern Florida, Bahamas, northern South America), depth (30–60 m), and behavior (solitary, rocky reef habitat). FishBase is authoritative on the family classification — LiveAquaria's listing of 'Grammidae' appears to be a retailer error.
